title: the mount — one <main> tag, a toast, and Prism from a CDN
the palette — the whole aesthetic lives in these CSS variables
four constants + two DOM handles — the first JS that runs
the prompt — meta moment: this is the prompt that generated the plan you're reading
code:
const PROMPT = `You're generating a reading plan for a "zen code reader" that walks me through a codebase one chunk at a time, in the order I should read to understand it.

Return ONLY a JSON array — no prose, no markdown fence. Each item:

{
  "title": "short label — where this fits in the story",
  "language": "js" | "ts" | "py" | "go" | "rs" | "..." (Prism language id),
  "code": "the exact source for this chunk, unmodified"
}

Rules:
- Order depth-first from the entry point: start with the top-level call site, then each function it calls, then their callees, etc.
- One chunk = one logical unit (a function, a class, a config block, an import group). Don't merge unrelated units; don't split a single function across chunks.
- Title should orient the reader — "entry point", "called by handleRequest to validate input", "helper used by render()". Not just the function name.
- Preserve code verbatim (whitespace, comments). No summarization, no "...". If a file is too large, pick the units that matter for understanding and say so in the title of the first chunk.
- If there are multiple entry points, cover one fully before starting the next.

Write the result to apps/zen-code/plans/<short-name>.json in this repo so I can upload it into the reader.`;

title: state boot — load from localStorage, fill in any missing fields
render() — the 2-line dispatcher that picks which screen to show
renderPaste() — textarea + upload button + the how-this-works reveal
loadPlanFromText() — one handler feeds both paste and file upload
parsePlan() — strips markdown fences first because AIs love wrapping JSON in ```
normalizeChunks() — accepts an array or {chunks:[...]}, drops items without code
copyPrompt() + showToast() — clipboard with a graceful fallback toast
renderReader() — the surface you're literally looking at right now
go() — navigation math; samples reading time on forward moves only
sampleCurrentChunk() — the outlier clamp (500ms–10min) that keeps your WPM honest
the ETA math — wpm, remaining words, minutes left
format helpers + the etaText toggle you tap to flip minutes-left vs finish-time
reset() — wipes the current plan but preserves your running WPM
keyboard — arrows + space, skipped when a text input has focus
